review:
Please please please remove hidden skill to place us in ranks beginning/mid season. It is impossible to consistently play with friend (who end in the same rank as I do with similar K/D and exact same win/loss ratio) when they start seasons/splits a whole rank and a half below me.
I remember playing placement matches back in the day and that system was far more consistent and better than whatever hidden metric you guys are using to decipher player skill.
If we are to stick with hidden skill, at the very least communicate with the player base what stats are weighed into it so that we can improve these metrics and maybe someday I can start ranked season/splits with literally the one and only guy I consistently play with. It just becomes too much of a grind when I hit the peak of my skill level as a casual player and I still have to grind my buddy up into where he's supposed to be. Just makes the gameplay loop almost intolerable sometimes.
Honestly, If this were to be fixed I would have next to no complaints about this game as a whole.

review:
Listen. LISTEN. I am not built for this game. I don't know who told me I was but they lied to my face and I'd like to find them.

Apex Legends is a perfectly good game being played by people who are not me. Respawn Entertainment clearly knew what they were doing when they built this thing, the movement is slick, the gunplay is tight, the hero abilities add a layer that keeps it from being just another battle royale. Objectively I can see that. Intellectually I understand it.

What I cannot do is survive longer than forty five seconds without getting absolutely cooked by someone who has clearly been playing since beta and has the kind of reaction time that should be studied by scientists. I drop in, I hear a noise, I turn around, I'm dead. Credits. Every time. Without fail. A masterclass in getting eliminated before I've even figured out which direction I'm facing.

The hero shooter angle is where it loses me on paper too. I'm not a battle royale guy to begin with and adding abilities on top of that is just giving the people who are already better than me more ways to embarrass me specifically.

Is it a good game? Probably yeah. Respawn built something that clearly works given how many people are still playing it years in. Is it a game for someone who gets motion sick watching their own killcam? Jury's out.

review:
I have played this game for far too long for the soul purpose of it being the only real place to use my favourite weapons since Titanfall 1 and 2 and while I am aware of Northstar client, I enjoyed playing apex with a few friends hence why I stuck with apex. Excluding origin client I have a few hours on this game on steam and I am willing to say a lot of that was enjoyed, but nowadays I only really play it because its a game me and my friends play.

They have changed my favourite gun recently, being the hemlock, I was under the impression it would be a temporary change, I hoped it would be because I detest the change, the whole reason I loved it is it was a 3 bullet burst assault rifle which is my favourite type of assault rifle irregardless of the game I am playing. And now it just feels like a reskinned flatline with a grenade launcher attachment. I will now out of stubbornness and spite refuse to use my favourite gun ever again. As I never cared if a weapon is good,  OP, meta or whatever, say for instance guns like the nemesis - I loathe its very existence I would rather go with merely my fists then equip the weapon. I simply just wanted to use what I enjoyed. And now what little enjoyment I had in this game is no longer accessible. First it was my favourite sight in the game, it was the 1x circular holo, now its not in the game outside of wildcard currently which I seldom play. Now its the hemlock, what next? going to take the prowler outback and give it full auto as well? oh wait that's already its hop up, what about the wingman? 6 round revolver- did you mean 5 or 12? Would rather the wingman only be able to have 6 shots total before reloading, if you miss 6 shots quite frankly you deserve what's coming to you.

I miss this game.

If you are of age between 12 and 17 with nothing but time, I would recomend this game, it's highly addictive. Imo, the best shooter battle royale with the best sound effects when downing or breaking shields. It's an amazing feeling to unload a scar and 100% to 0% someone. Nothing beats that. 

But then as you get older, you don't have as much time to play the game, and if you don't keep up, you'll be left behind quite quickly. I love this game, it has its ups and downs. for example:

The downs:

- Infestation of hackers. Its always nice to go into a lobby with a Rampart unload 100% accuracy or freaking L-Stars that don't miss a single shot. I found a couple of them in SILVER LOBBIES when climbing up and much after the season reset, and always a low level account. Which is amazing. Great feeling.

- Broken step sounds. I seriously don't know why they say "We fixed it" but then the step sound is worse than before. I hear them coming on the right, below me, but somehow they are above me. Could be skill issue, sure, but tons of streamers complained about it so I'm not alone here.

- New Legends, broken kits, as per usual

The Ups:

- Fun game.

review:
Ranking Apex: 7.6/10
TLDR: Good game overall; hard to pick up/hard to put down

Core Game Mode: 8/10
Battle royal modes are great, definitely want to play with another person tho. 

Ease of Access: 4/10
While the game is incredible and relatively inviting to new players, the games depth, core mechanic, and mass mobility make it difficult for newer players to pick up and learn. I luckily have played several fps before. Not good for beginners to the genre. The welcome quest line is incredible tho for teaching players to do more things,
NOTE; I have so so so so so so so so so much resect for games who lock competitive behind an actually challenging questline. I feel like more games should do this.

Monetization: 6/10
Like many games, it's free to play (Wow how surprising(sarcasm)) and has mass monetization. I've started playing at the end of a season so I haven't yet felt the need to purchase anything. If I continue on further through this game I will prob buy a Battle pass or smt.

Progession: 3/10
I'm fine with the fact it takes awhile to unlock characters, BUT the fact i'm 15-ish hours in and I haven't gotten a single new character (Aka the characters other than the starting ones) is just sad.

review:
Current Status of Apex Legends:
Community Sentiment: Many long-time players feel that the game's focus has shifted towards profit, particularly with the increase in expensive cosmetics, rather than fixing core game issues.
Player Base: While still popular, the game has seen a drop from over 400,000 to around 100,000 active users on Steam within a year, according to player reports.
Performance: Players frequently report issues with matchmaking, audio bugs, and cheaters, which contribute to the perception of a declining game.
Content Concerns: Many users feel the game has become stale, with fewer new characters (legends) and a lack of fresh, engaging content.
Verdict: While the game is not completely dead, it is definitely in a challenging phase of its life cycle, with many long-time players stepping away.

review: 🏆 Apex Legends: Still the King of Movement Shooters (2026 Review)Rating: 9.5/10After years of consistent updates, Apex Legends remains, without a doubt, the most fluid, exciting, and polished battle royale on the market. It successfully combines the fast-paced action of a hero shooter with the tactical intensity of a battle royale, resulting in an unmatched FPS experience.Here is why it is still worth your time:Unmatched Gunplay & Movement: The mechanics feel just right. The sliding, climbing, and traversal are silky smooth, making every encounter feel fast and skill-driven.The Best Communication System in Gaming: The ping system is revolutionary. It allows you to communicate with teammates in detail without ever needing a mic, making it incredibly accessible for solo queue players.Deep Hero Variety: With a massive roster of Legends—each with unique abilities—you can truly choose a character that fits your playstyle, whether you are aggressive, defensive, or supportive.Constant Evolution: The team at Respawn keeps the game fresh with regular seasons, new map changes, and evolving meta, ensuring that the gameplay rarely feels repetitive.Fair Free-to-Play Model: It is astounding that a game of this AAA quality is free to play. The monetization is largely cosmetic, and you can absolutely enjoy top-tier gameplay without spending a dime.Final Verdict:Whether you are a newcomer or a returning veteran, Apex Legends offers an electrifying experience that keeps you coming back for "just one more match." It is a masterpiece of modern multiplayer gaming.

review:
Amazing Game IMO! One of THE best battle royale style shooters out there. Characters are fun to play, the game is free, you can play with up to 2 of your friends on a team. They added permanent evo shields now, meaning you never have to search for one, EVO harvesters spawn on the map letting you upgrade to blue if you haven't run into a fight. They also added ability upgrades when you reach Blue and Purple shield, meaning you can augment each kit further than the base. They added a UAV style Survey Beacon for Recon legends, so you can see teams around you OR on a Control legend you have access to Ring Consoles (allowing you to see the next location of ring close). Both of these are a boost to different styles of play. You can play aggressive or defensively and still have an enjoyable experience. They balanced weaker weapons with an Akimbo dual wield option if you find and pick up 2 or upgrade them at gun caches.

TLDR: 9.6/10 Awesome battle royale style 3v3 shooter, would recommend.

review:
Definitely a game with its ups and downs. For those on controller you better be playing "claw" or with paddles as there are many buttons you must press whilst looking around. Apex's gameplay is catered to being competitive, so the TDM features will be a bit dull to most, especially Fortnite fans. 

Apex is full of exciting characters to master with each truly feeling unique compared to others. I am an enthusiast on learning the ins-outs of characters, and this games provides plenty of that to do. The content creating community is full of knowledge that will help you improve in the game.

If you want to play ranked you can more likely solo to diamond but no-one will blame you if you cant solo to master as [b]the game requires networking with friends to keep your sanity into high ranks[/b]. I have reached masters more than 10 seasons in this game and have really enjoyed every climb.

My biggest gripes about the game are audio, balancing, and currently its major focus on movement being the end all be all answer to fixing its legends. There will be times where you will want to take a break from the game, just take the break its fine, life is more important and this game consumes alot of your time.

That being said Apex is my favorite game and I hope you enjoy it as much as I do. If you want to play with me, you better be good.

review:
My wife is taking testosterone and it is tormenting me

My wife (31F) and I (30M) have been together for 5 years. We have two small children, a 4-year-old and a 2-year-old. Our life has always been stable, until about six months ago.

She decided to start taking testosterone on her own. She started following some "biohacking lifestyle" influencers and became convinced that testosterone was the key to having the perfect body and unshakable confidence. She didn't see a serious endocrinologist; She got the contact of a “coach” who can get the recipes. She says it's for "performance" and curiosity, to see how far her body goes.

The problem is that the price of this is our family.

Her patience with the children simply disappeared. She was always the sweet mother, who sat on the floor to play. Now, if our 4-year-old cries or throws a tantrum, she gets angry. Her tone changed; it's more serious, drier. The other day, I saw her yelling at him in such an intimidating way that I had to intervene and take the kids to their room. She is constantly on edge.

And there's the part that I'm even embarrassed to write, but I need to say it. Her body is changing in ways that are destroying our intimacy. There are changes down there... anatomical changes that I prefer not to describe in detail, but that made everything very strange to me. She is no longer the woman I know. Her scent changed completely; It's a strong, masculine odor that takes over the room.

Her libido is insatiable, but in an aggressive and purely physical way. There is no more affection, there is no foreplay. It's like she's using my body for chemical relief. If I don't respond, she humiliates me. She says I'm "weak", that I don't have energy, that I'm "less of a man" than her.

I clean the house, take care of the children and still have to walk on eggshells so as not to trigger a tantrum in her. She is obsessed with the gym, spends hours there and comes back even more exhausted.

When I try to suggest that she stop, or that this is affecting our marriage, she says that I'm jealous of her "evolution" and that I'm insecure because I can't handle an "alpha woman."

I love the mother she was. I miss her softness, her smell, the way we connected. Now, I look at her and see a strange, twisted, angry version of the person I promised to love. I'm afraid of what this will do to our children if it continues like this, but at the same time, I'm afraid of filing for divorce and leaving them alone with this "new version" of her half the time.

I feel completely alone in this. Being in a marriage where you feel disgusted and afraid of the person who should be your safe haven is a loneliness I wouldn't wish on anyone.

review:
Apex Legends – Request for South African Servers

As a dedicated Apex Legends player from South Africa, I’d like to raise an important issue that affects not only myself but a large portion of the African player base — the lack of local servers.

Currently, South African players are forced to connect to European or Middle Eastern servers, which results in consistently high ping (usually 150–200ms or higher). This creates a noticeable disadvantage in gameplay, including delayed hit registration, lag during fights, and an overall less competitive experience. In a fast-paced game like Apex Legends, where timing and precision are everything, this puts us at a significant disadvantage compared to players with lower latency.

The Apex Legends community in South Africa and across Africa has grown massively over the past few years. Many players are passionate, competitive, and committed to the game, but the server limitations make it difficult to fully enjoy or compete at a high level. Introducing South African servers would not only improve gameplay quality but also strengthen and grow the player base in this region.

Other major games have already implemented African servers, showing that the infrastructure and demand are there. Adding local servers would demonstrate that Apex Legends values its global community and is willing to invest in underserved regions.

I strongly urge the Apex Legends team to consider adding South African servers. It would make a huge difference to thousands of players and significantly improve the overall experience.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

review:
It can be hit or miss, as there are more sweats and veteran players than new players. 

The best part is the wildcard mode, which is a quicker version of the regular battle royale mod that lets you respawn immediately after death instead of waiting for a revive. Then there are rotating modes as gun game (called gun run), capture the flag (control), and team death match. 

Honestly, the only bad thing about the game is the regular battle-royale mode and it's ranked counterpart, as it requires more patience, probably more walking than shooting, and the high chance of dying before doing anything meaningful. If there is a reason to download and play this game, it's all the other modes. 

Like every other game, it has microtransactions that are cosmetic. Up to you if it's worth buying or ignoring, but it has no effect on gameplay.

review:
Ah yes, *Apex Legends* — the only game scientifically engineered to turn “one quick match” into a 3-hour emotional breakdown.

The matchmaking system is incredible. It sees you miss half a magazine and thinks, “Yeah, this guy clearly belongs with predators.” Suddenly you’re in a lobby where people don’t walk — they *slide, jump, spin,* and somehow headshot you mid-air while filing their taxes.

Your teammates are even better. One is roleplaying a tourist, admiring the scenery 500 meters away. The other hot-drops into a warzone, gets knocked in 0.7 seconds, and immediately spam-pings like you’re legally obligated to save them. Meanwhile, you’re still looking for a weapon that isn’t a Mozambique and a dream.

Gunfights are a masterpiece of suspense. You empty an entire mag into someone, see 27 hit markers, and the game goes, “Cute. Anyway, you’re dead.” Kill cams should just say: *“Trust me bro, he hit you.”*

And the servers? Phenomenal. Nothing says “competitive integrity” like enemies teleporting like they unlocked fast travel mid-fight. You’re not lagging — you’re just experiencing *advanced movement mechanics.*

But don’t worry, the skins are amazing. Because if you’re going to get absolutely deleted in 2 seconds, you might as well look like a limited-edition clown while doing it.

In the end, *Apex Legends* isn’t about winning. It’s about personal growth. Specifically, growing your tolerance for pain, confusion, and the uncontrollable urge to say, “one more game” like you haven’t learned anything at all.

review:
Apex Legends is one of the most polished and exciting battle royale games available today. From the moment you drop into a match, the game delivers a fast-paced, high-energy experience that keeps you constantly engaged. Unlike many other games in the genre, Apex stands out because of its smooth movement system, unique character abilities, and strong emphasis on teamwork.

One of the best aspects of Apex Legends is its roster of “Legends,” each with their own distinct abilities and playstyles. Whether you prefer aggressive pushes, defensive setups, or supporting your team, there’s always a character that fits your approach. This adds a layer of strategy that goes beyond just aiming and shooting, making every match feel different depending on your squad composition.

The gunplay is another major highlight. Weapons feel responsive, balanced, and satisfying to use. Combined with the game’s fluid movement mechanics—like sliding, climbing, and ziplining—combat feels dynamic and skill-based. Winning a fight isn’t just about who shoots first; positioning, movement, and teamwork all play a huge role.

Apex also excels in its ping system, which allows players to communicate effectively without needing a microphone. This feature makes the game much more accessible and reduces the frustration of playing with random teammates. It’s one of the best communication systems in any multiplayer game and has influenced many others since its release.

Visually, the game looks great, with detailed environments and vibrant maps that keep things interesting. The developers regularly update the game with new content, including Legends, weapons, and map changes, which helps keep the experience fresh over time.

That said, the game isn’t perfect. Like many competitive online games, it can sometimes suffer from server issues, matchmaking inconsistencies, or frustrating teammates. Additionally, the learning curve can be steep for new players, especially when going up against experienced opponents.

Overall, Apex Legends is a top-tier battle royale that successfully blends fast gameplay, strategic depth, and strong team mechanics. Whether you’re playing casually or competitively, it offers an experience that’s both fun and rewarding. It’s definitely worth playing if you enjoy shooters with skill-based movement and team coordination.

Rating: 9/10

review:
Imagine being a Season 0 vet, sinking mass amounts of money and hours into liking a game so much, that the developers get hyucked into the "cash cow" position rather than the "quality assurance" section, to then proceed to look at the cosmetic and competitive side of the game and cater to them exclusively, only for me to hit 1000 hours on a game that looks and plays like nothing you used to play it for: a casual BR with fun movement, satisfying gunplay, and overall, hilarity between friends for the moments you genuinely enjoyed, rather than the extremely competitive settings, configs, controller aim and sweaty gameplay this poor game has been subjected to now (I literally have INVINITE more excitement in Deadlock than this game, and I usually avoid MOBAs, what with my lack of skill and luck in the genre, and yet, League is one of the many irritating games that is STILL in my Love/Hate relationship list!).

I wish I could love this game again, and yes, I admit that even though I have hours and levels in the game, I don't enjoy having a game come and go so fast with zero progression in a singular 1v1. It's also really sad that I don't look at the insane roster of characters and loadouts of weapons to the point where I question the standings of "who is my main?" or "what guns do I genuinely adore?", rather than going with the braindead "Who and What is meta nowadays and how effective can I get a win?" instead of focusing on FUN. This has been a long standing issue with games like this, much like League, where half of the roster or half of the weaponry don't matter and if you aren't using the best of the best, then you're gonna die quick.

Also, the sheer difference from Season 0-4 all the way to Season 5-Now is practically astonishing when it comes to how much I've been reduced to a pile of washed up garbage. I used to be a casual lover of this game, now I don't even want to touch it with a 30ft. pole, what with all the sweaty tryhards in the casual lobbies competing like it was their last days on Earth.

Make no mistake, I have skill issues with this game, but exactly the same way I feel about particular games of this calibre, if you don't keep consistantly grinding and giving up sleep and cleanliness altogether, there is no point to even continuing your journey with this game. I would have to guess it's why we have nothing but smurfs nowadays, let alone cheaters or players on PCs with controllers letting the game play FOR them.

Sorry Respawn, you had something great that this guy enjoyed genuinely back then, but now rage at the fact that I'm no longer playing the Apex Legends I knew and loved so dearly back then, and instead, I'm playing the poorly performed swan-song of Titanfall 3 and it's remaining qualities that continue to be replace and rotted away with "Original" content and mechanics that just should've been dead a long time ago and made into a new game that would deliver the exact same thing, albeit without a bunch of loot boxes, crossovers and not to mention, EA's finest technique: Microtransactions.

Season 0-28 veteran, signing off. Enjoy the thousands I sank into this poor excuse of a BR that ain't as magical as it used to be, EA. You forced these guys to ditch an original idea for a game that you milked excessively. RIP Respawn's casual community and developers, up yours to the EA big heads that continue to make deals just to make enough money to make a T-Shirt.

review:
I have 98 hours of play time in Apex according to Steam, but I have played since the very beginning of its release through the EA launcher in the past. I took a long break, and recently came back to the game given its recent upsurge in popularity. I wasn't expecting much to be honest, as most large-free games nowadays sell out and don't do a great job of balancing their games, but I was pleasantly surprised to see the current state of Apex.

The first thing I noticed when I started playing again is how incredibly well optimized this game is on pc. I'm sure about the other systems, but this game runs better for me than COUNTER-STRIKE, which is an absolutely insane thing to say, and I can't believe I just said that, but it's actually true. I averaged higher FPS on Apex with max settings than I did with the buggy ahh overflow-prone graphics of counter-strike on ALL LOW SETTINGS. Apex runs so incredibly well; I've literally never been more pleasantly surprised by a game's performance.

Second, I have ran into very few to no cheaters in my games so far. I'm still trying to climb back up the ranks as I'm still rusty, but I still feel I've played enough to say that the cheater problem has greatly improved. 

Also, Apex is surprisingly well balanced. Despite a couple of weapons that have remained quite unpopular over the years(Charge rifle ._.), you can [for the most part] pick any character in the game, select any loadout you want, and pop off if you're good enough and have the right positions. The physics are still fun, being able to craft banners is incredible, and Gibby FINALLY GOT BUFFED--but I still have a couple of glaring problems with this game.

The most glaring problem for me personally is the AUDIO. IT'S ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE. The audio is incredibly inaccurate, inconsistent, or just outright doesn't exist when someone is spearheaded down from the sky to your exact location. The audio is just LOUD, that's it. Half the time, you can just completely disregard audio in a combat situation, as you just can't trust that what you hear will give you the info you need. I haven't dealt with such poor audio in any other game.

A close second place issue is the maps: they pretty much all suck. Some maps have less issues, but some have horrible choke points and extremely difficult rotation positions, getting you possibly killed if the zone appears at the opposite side of the map. Also, there are some zones on some maps that are literally impossible to play. Recently, I played a game of King's Row where the final circle was split in half by a giant mountain. It was so tall that you couldn't even make it over with an evac tower, and so thick that not even Alter could make a portal through it. All you could do was go through storm, which was a guaranteed death sentence. All you could do was wait for the circle to shrink to one of the two halves, killing whoever is on the other side. There was quite literally nothing that could be done. I could go on about map issues, but that would take far too long.

There are some smaller issues that make the game annoying to play for me personally, but may not apply to everyone, so those I will leave out. 

This review may seem more negative than positive, but that's just how I do criticism. Overall, Apex is incredibly fun, and I would recommend anyone to download it and give it a shot. It's not everybody's kind of game, but if it does turn out to be, then you're sure to LOVE IT. (Also what did yall do to the hemlock? ik it was op after the rework but man it got nerfed so bad LMAO)

review:
How to have more free time in your life.

1.Open Steam
Launch the Steam application on your computer and log into your account.
    
2.Go to Your Library
Click on the “Library” tab at the top of the window. This shows all your installed games.
    
3.Find Apex Legends
Scroll through your game list and locate Apex Legends.
    
4.Right-click the Game
Right-click on Apex Legends in your library list.
    
5.Select Manage
In the dropdown menu, hover over “Manage.”
    
6.Click Uninstall
Click “Uninstall” from the options.
    
7.Confirm Removal
A confirmation window will appear. Click “Uninstall” again to remove the game from your system.

review:
Apex Legends is an exceptional fusion of tactical intellect and mechanical precision. Rather than relying solely on gunplay, the game demands a sophisticated blend of sharp reflexes, strategic foresight, spatial awareness, and seamless teamwork.

Each match unfolds with unpredictable intensity, where calculated decisions, coordinated communication, and mastery of unique character abilities determine survival. The fluid movement system and dynamic combat create an experience that is both mentally stimulating and physically demanding.
What distinguishes Apex Legends is its ability to challenge the player’s cognitive skills as much as their reflexes, ensuring that no encounter feels repetitive or mundane.

Rating: 9/10

review:
Apex Legends is a fast-paced battle royale developed by Respawn Entertainment. It stands out with its smooth movement, team-based gameplay, and unique “Legend” abilities that add depth beyond typical shooters.

Each character has special skills that encourage strategy and coordination, making teamwork essential to winning. The ping system is also excellent, allowing communication even without voice chat.

The gunplay feels responsive and satisfying, but the game can be challenging for beginners due to skilled opponents and fast combat. Updates and new content keep the experience fresh, though occasional balance issues can appear.

Overall, Apex Legends is one of the best battle royale games for players who enjoy speed, teamwork, and skill-based gameplay.

Some say that The Finals is bad because they used siri tier "AI" voice acting while licensing the voice actors voice for it, missing that the entire reason why "AI" sucks is copyright infringement. review:
Am I good at this game? ♥♥♥♥ no. 

Do I enjoy it? Yes.

Very fun with friends, especially when you get 3 stacked as silvers into plat lobbies and start yelling at each other.
The people complaining about expensive cosmetics fail to realize they're purely optional, and while EA is making all the hype about these overpriced skins and heirlooms, the playerbase and community itself still revolves around the gameplay. You'll have a lot of learning to do in order to actually get good at the game, more than I could ever take the time to do, but once you successfully get consistent lurch strafes, b-hops, and zipline shenanigans, you feel invincible.
Recently, I feel the game getting a bit better from Respawn's end as well with the roadmap, changes to guns and legends, almost like they actually care... scary. Anyways, play until you unlock ranked and play a few ranked games. If you still feel its not your thing, feel free to quit... but I think you'll find that hard to do :P 

Have fun!

review:
I came back to this game after its launch, 6 years later. Guess what I find?
Me, being a casual, low skill gamer, forced to pair up with incompetent teammates against no-life players; OR, paired with competent players against EVEN HARSHER no-life players.
'Skill' distribution in matchmaking is so broken because of these 2 factors alone that I have a 150 match losing streak on one character. The variance between skills of competing teams can be +/- 30%, meaning you can be playing in the lowest brackets and playing against the average or superior players.
Game still forces you to start matches with incomplete teams even though only 1% of games are won this way. You can't leave a match once matchmaking has started; you have to wait until after character selection, so you can end up wasting a minute of your life multiple times over because the game won't let you stop whenever YOU want, even when you're down BOTH teammates.
Players can quit without any real form of punishment. Empty slots aren't forced to be refilled and you don't get an option to just rejoin a queue rather than start at a disadvantage.
A frankly absurd amount of players go AFK, teammates or enemies, and the game doesn't boot them after even 30 seconds.
THE LOWEST SKILL BRACKETS ARE FILLED WITH ULTRA PRECISE ACCURACY PLAYERS! MAKE IT MAKE SENSE!
No penalties for players who split up because they NEED to get their specific loadout or chase kills.
No penalties for players/teams who die in the first minute of the match starting because they want to jump immediately or go to high competition areas to start getting kills immediately. YOU want to do that? Fine. But if you die, it should cost you XP getting taken off your character or forcing longer wait times for screwing over your teammates, especially when it causes a team wipe so soon into the match.
People who have mics: 80% have noise in the background or static, those who actually talk will trash talk you just for not following quickly enough.
Placing higher doesn't earn nearly enough rewards for the accomplishment that is.
You can't auto skip the victory scene. I know I lost, let's get on with it so I can get to my next match. This isn't a tournament.
Too many players get downed and have to wait for revive, only to quit because they lost the gear they had or don't trust their teammates to win, OR, the players are taking too long to respawn or making no effort to.
Matches can take over a minute to start sometimes. Why? This game uses bots in matches, PUT THEM IN and let me play against my level.
This is going to be the last competitive game I play most likely, just because no developer wants to balance their matches, and clearly never play enough to be insulted by how matchmaking treats them. The game itself is fine, but because of how you're matched up, you don't want to play unless you have naturally high skill in this game, so new/returning players are always cheated of fun to let high skill players have fun at your expense.
And the Micro transaction/battlepass/cosmetic rewards are hyper predatory. I won't bother ranting about what we all know and hate.

review:
Played this game for over a 2000+ hours if you include console. Starting with the positive

Yes, you will have fun playing this game. The legends are actually quite interesting and everyone gravitates towards their favorite given the playstyle, lore, and aesthetics. There is a lot more of a high skill ceiling for this game, along with not being the worst choice if you're just getting it for the first time with some friends

My issues arise with the fact that EA doesn't listen to the community and is extremely money hungry. The battle passes have somehow got worse, scummier, way more in your face, more expensive, and just not worth it anymore. Well maybe aesthetics don't matter too much to you, and that makes sense, can't mess up an amazing battle royale right? EA comes to prove you wrong! Apex had (has?) the best gameplay for a battle royale imo. I've been playing on and off since it's release, and they perfected it around Season 7. For some unholy reason, they decided to remove regular duos in favor of the tiktok version of Apex so It's over within minutes, they have YET to listen to the community about solos, and they have shown time and time again that they just don't listen to the community. Whether it comes to maps, lore (seriously tf is up with the lore?), weapons, game modes, etc. They just DON'T keep what's fun or already good. It's this constant slap in the face to keep the "Happy woah, new thing!" feeling even though It's already milked dry.

Yes, you will be frustrated because a Wraith will be your random, rush a squad, die, and leave within seconds, but that's not entirely the fault of EA or Respawn. What makes me sadly not recommend this, is that they've had YEARS, had the PERFECT recipe for success, and just kinda... tossed it away with little to no hope that they'll listen to anyone. But don't worry! 3 more Wattson and Wraith skins coming your way! Also we added some random expensive currency for animated skins! There's 3 different tiers to the same battle pass because we can't be bothered to just make one very good pass that more people would buy!

I think you will have fun playing this game if you've never played before, although I would heavily recommend playing with friends. If you're returning, you will have some fun, some nostalgia, and you might move on because admittedly it is VERY different than what it was. If you want fast shooting gameplay, you will have fun in Mixtape. Personally though,  Apex at It's BEST is trios (Or old duos) with your friends. That is the Apex I will recommend to people, convince my friends to play, and spend money on. But that Apex Isn't really here anymore, and especially with the removal of duos, It's even less likely that I'd have 2 friends online that are willing to play this at the same time. Just another story all too similar, that It's nostalgic, you might have some fun, but It's ultimately a shame that it ended up what it is today.

Short version: 
If you start playing now, expect that you are not welcome in this game. You will get killed by laserbeams of bullets the moment you poke your head out because of people that are chasing numbers rather than enjoyment. I strongly recommend other FPS games first. 

Long version:
I dont really have problem with the game itself. Its mid, not offering much really. A generic hero shooter in the BR format (which is boring too, but whateves). The way legends are locked is also sad.
But hey, i could get past that, since at least 2 legends you get from start can be useful. But a bad start nontheless.

But by the players i am encountering from the start, im guessing the game wants me to quit. Because everyone i meet laserbeams me from any given distance, with no shots missed whatsoever. No matter the level or the gun, i dont get a chance to fight back. I know im average in FPS games, and i focus on variety rather than one, but do i really deserve to play against people with 500+, 1000+ hours on this game from the start?

I dont know what kind of matchmaking manipulation is the game doing in unranked, but clearly its failing miserably if the goal is to even out the playing field. But im sure there is some inferior system compared to "ping is king" system, which so far hasnt failed me. This one has.

Im gonna keep playing, but i will focus on unlocking ranked and hopefully, FINALLY, for once get to play with some casuals down there in da bronze. Hopefully that will actually give interesting gunfights, instead of it being over before it even starts. I would really like to play the game and not be a feeding stock, thats all.

Im gonna actually allow comments in case someone has some words to say to me. But keep in mind i will be checking your hours in case you do, to see if i should take you seriously or ignore you. (Privated profile means you go in the tryhards bag ;) mkay?).

review:
**Review: Apex Legends – A Fast-Paced and Skill-Intensive Battle Royale Experience**

Apex Legends is one of the most exciting and dynamic first-person shooter games available today. Developed by Respawn Entertainment, the game offers a unique battle royale experience that combines fast gameplay, strategic teamwork, and diverse character abilities.

In *Apex Legends*, players compete in teams of three, with a total of 20 teams in each match. The objective is simple: be the last team standing. However, the gameplay itself is far from simple. Each player selects a character, known as a “Legend,” and every Legend has unique skills and abilities. This adds a strong layer of strategy, as team composition and skill usage can greatly influence the outcome of each match.

The game features multiple modes to keep players engaged. In the standard “unranked” mode, players can freely choose when to jump from the dropship and land in different locations across the map. From there, they must quickly find weapons, gear, and resources while fighting off other teams. For players seeking a more competitive experience, the ranked mode introduces a tier system, starting from Bronze and progressing through Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Master, and finally Predator. This mode challenges players to continuously improve their skills and climb the ranks.

One of the most unique aspects of *Apex Legends* is its special “wild card” or event modes. The game frequently collaborates with different fictional franchises, such as *Final Fantasy* or *Gundam*, introducing exclusive weapons, skins, and gameplay changes. These limited-time events bring fresh content and keep the game exciting, especially with regular updates and balance adjustments.

The standout feature of *Apex Legends* is undoubtedly its gunplay system. The mechanics of shooting, movement, and character control are highly refined and require significant practice to master. Compared to many other first-person shooters, this game is considered one of the most challenging. However, this difficulty is also what makes it so appealing. Winning a fight feels incredibly rewarding because it reflects real improvement in skill.

In terms of the in-game community, players can either play solo and be matched with random teammates or team up with friends. Communication varies—some players cooperate well, while others may not use voice or text chat at all. For the best experience, playing with friends is highly recommended, as teamwork is essential in this game.

*Apex Legends* is free to play, making it accessible to everyone. Cosmetic items such as skins can be obtained through the in-game reward system or purchased directly. These items do not affect gameplay, ensuring a fair competitive environment.

Overall, *Apex Legends* is a highly engaging and challenging game that stands out in the battle royale genre. It is perfect for players who enjoy competitive gameplay, teamwork, and skill-based progression. Despite its steep learning curve, it offers a thrilling experience that keeps players coming back for more.

RECETA DE HOT CAKES FLAMIN' HOT:

Prepara hot cakes Flamin' Hot triturando 3/4 de bolsa de Cheetos Flamin' Hot hasta hacerlos polvo y mezclándolos con 1.5 tazas de harina, 1.5 cucharaditas de polvo para hornear, 1/2 cucharadita de sal, leche y huevo. Ingredientes

Instrucciones

review:
Apex Legends is what happens when a battle royale drinks five energy drinks and decides to become a superhero movie. You drop in with two random teammates: one thinks they’re an esports prodigy, and the other is emotionally attached to looting bins 3 kilometers away.

The gunplay is smoother than someone who says “trust me” right before ruining your life. Movement is so good that after sliding down a hill once, every other game feels like walking through wet cement.

The legends themselves are great. Want to heal? There’s a medic. Want to scan enemies? There’s a tracker. Want to throw gas into a room and become a war criminal? They’ve got you covered.

Communication is amazing thanks to the ping system, which lets you say “enemy here,” “loot there,” and “why are you both dead already?” without using a mic.

Of course, no Apex match is complete without spending 15 minutes gearing up, hitting one incredible shot, then getting instantly deleted by a sweaty Wraith moving at the speed of Wi-Fi.

Overall: 9/10. Incredible gameplay, chaotic teamwork, and enough emotional damage to keep you coming back daily.

review: Started this game on xbox back in season 2. Had a blast even if i wasnt good at it. after almost a year of playing, got burnt out and took a bit of a break for a few seasons. Came back when games popularity really started to surge, started grinding ranked and genuinely enjoyed it until they changed the ranked system. No more placements. Take an even longer journey through rookie, then bronze, then silver, etc. Everyone thrown together at the, low ranks and high ranks. Having to wait a couple of days before even thinking about touching ranked again. Then I took another break after the game no longer felt fun again, came back with many friends, grinded out again, got a bunch of heirlooms, (that i paid for), and find out someone else connected my xbox account to their steam account, and now i cant connect my steam. Weeks of trying to contact EA, (who dont have a customer support line, email, etc.). Now if I ever want to use those heirlooms again, i have to hook up my series X and pay for gold again. Not to also mention more ranked changes, increasing rp penalties, huge increase in cheaters, laggy servers, etc. I feel like I'm playing Destiny 2 all over again, a great game, let down by its developer team. To sum it up, this game is wasted potential. I'm holding out for Titanfall 3.

review:
Easy Anti-Cheat blocks Apex Legends on CachyOS (Linux)

I am running Apex Legends on Linux using CachyOS (Arch-based distribution) with a custom kernel (BORE + LTO) and an AMD GPU (RX 6600).

The game fails to launch and shows the error:
“Wine, Proton, and Steam Deck are not supported by this application.”

This indicates that Easy Anti-Cheat (EAC) is not initializing correctly under Proton.

Important notes:

Steam is installed natively (not Flatpak)
Tested with Proton Experimental and GE-Proton (latest versions)
System is fully updated

This issue appears to be related to kernel-level compatibility or EAC detection, rather than user misconfiguration.

Linux gaming has improved significantly, but cases like this still block users on valid setups. CachyOS focuses on performance optimizations, and it would be great to see broader compatibility with such environments.

Request:
Please improve EAC support and Proton compatibility for Linux systems using custom kernels, or provide clearer guidance on supported configurations.

Thank you.

review:
yeah… this game is 90% decision-making and 10% aim, no matter what people say.

everyone thinks it’s about cracked movement and one-clipping people, but most fights are decided before the first shot. bad positioning, late rotates, taking a fight you didn’t need to take that’s what gets you sent back to lobby over and over.

early on you blame teammates. after a few thousand hours you realize most of your deaths are your fault. swung too wide, stayed too long, didn’t reset, didn’t armor swap fast enough, didn’t leave when the fight was already lost.

gunskill matters, but not as much as people think. you can beam someone and still lose because you took a stupid angle or got third partied 5 seconds later. that’s just how the game works.

some things that never change no matter how long you play:

don’t overcommit. if a fight feels weird, it probably is
reset early. people wait way too long to heal and it gets them killed
positioning > aim. high ground and cover win fights
armor swaps save more fights than heals
if a fight takes too long, expect a third party. always
don’t ego chall everything just because you cracked someone

also movement is overrated if you don’t know when to use it. yeah it looks cool, but half the time simple strafing and good cover does more than flying around like a maniac and getting one-clipped mid-slide.

ranked especially teaches you patience. you don’t need to take every fight. sometimes the best play is just leaving. most people never learn that.

after this long the game isn’t really about winning every match, it’s about consistency. making fewer bad decisions than the other squad.

and yeah… you still get rolled sometimes and there’s nothing you could’ve done. that part never changes either.

review:
9/10 
200$ FOR A HEIRLOOM

-An incredible battle royale with some of the best movement and gunplay in the genre. Every match feels fast, skill-based, and rewarding, especially when playing legends like Wraith or Ash. The mechanics, team play, and overall polish make it easy to sink hundreds of hours into the game without getting bored.

However, there’s one major downside: heirlooms. While they look amazing and feel special, the amount of money you need to spend to guarantee one is just too high. It takes away from an otherwise near-perfect experience.

Overall, Apex Legends is a top-tier game, but the heirloom system holds it back from being a 10/10.

review:
I've played this game casually on and off through different patches. I've enjoyed it so far, but the past few months have offered the best experience.

Back then, this game felt unfair for casual players like me. Skirmishes felt like it's decided by luck, whether you found good armor (+helmet) or not, and you're guaranteed to lose otherwise. Queue time was extremely long, to a point where I found myself spending more time in the lobby queueing, if not running around the map hoping to find a good loot. 

From what I experience, this isn't the case anymore. Queue time makes sense now, and there's no bot anymore, at least in my skill level matchmaking. There's also less armor diff, i.e. >85% of the time you won't find people with an armor two levels above you, unless you're afk essentially. This change really encourages gunfight while still making it fair and incentivizes good mechanics instead of luck.

review:
"This game is absolute garbage now."

It was never perfect, but the game was actually peak back in 2023. At least characters were still fun to play then. Now, everyone is nerfed into the ground. I tried coming back after a break, and it’s a total nightmare. The game is filled with movement sweats who beam you from across the map before you even see them. It's definitely not beginner-friendly or returning-player-friendly anymore.

And what the hell is a 30-player lobby for Duos? 15 squads on these massive maps is a joke. You spend the whole game running around without seeing a single soul, and when you finally encounter someone, they play like literal gods. Is the game so dead that you can't even fill a proper lobby anymore? It’s pathetic.

The worst part is that there are almost no other fast-paced battle royales like this. PUBG is a game for grandpas, and Fortnite is just like a ponytail version of PUBG. But that doesn't give you the right to ruin this game.

Breakable glass? Is that your "big innovation"? What a joke. Also, there is nothing more disgusting than having to empty your bank account just for a single skin. EA needs to stop obsessing over selling overpriced skins and actually focus on fixing the gameplay.

Bring back the original map with the old construction buildings and stop forcing these trash maps down our throats. Apex is just a cheap, watered-down copy of Titanfall anyway. Just roll the game back to the 2023 version and stop ruining it.

review:
A Perfect Blend of Chaos and Slick Movement

If you've ever wondered what would happen if you threw Fortnite, PUBG, and Overwatch into a blender, Apex Legends is the result. It takes the gritty survival and looting of PUBG, the fast-paced energy of Fortnite (thankfully, without the building), and the hero-shooter team synergy of Overwatch, creating a battle royale that feels incredibly fresh.

What makes it great:

Movement is King: The core mechanics here are incredibly smooth. Mastering the movement—sliding, climbing, and repositioning—is the absolute key to winning gunfights. If you have good movement, you can outplay almost anyone.

Balanced Roster: The character abilities (Legends) are surprisingly well-balanced. They add a fantastic tactical layer to team fights without feeling completely overpowered. Gun skill still matters most.

High Fun Factor: The fast-paced core gameplay loop is just undeniably enjoyable and highly addictive.

You have to accept that a huge part of this game relies heavily on RNG and luck. Your success often depends on what weapons you manage to find right after dropping, how favorably the zone sets up for your team, and the constant, looming threat of being third-partied.

While the heavy RNG and getting shot in the back can be frustrating at times, the phenomenal gunplay, deep movement mechanics, and team strategy make it all worth it. It’s a highly enjoyable, chaotic battle royale that constantly keeps you on your toes. Highly recommended if you want a fast, competitive shooter!

review:
👍 Recommended (but not perfect)

Apex Legends is one of the best battle royale games out there, but it definitely has its issues.

The good:

Movement and gunplay feel amazing and really smooth
Each Legend has unique abilities that make the game more strategic
Ping system is one of the best in any multiplayer game
Fast-paced matches that don’t feel boring
Regular updates and new content keep things fresh

The bad:

Matchmaking can feel unfair, especially for newer players
Teammates can make or break your experience (solo queue can be rough)
Sweaty lobbies can get frustrating
Occasional server issues and lag
Can feel repetitive after a long time

Overall, Apex Legends is a super fun and polished game if you enjoy competitive shooters, but it can be frustrating depending on matchmaking and teammates.

Final verdict: great game, just don’t expect every match to be fun 😅

review:
The core of Apex Legends remains a masterpiece of movement and gunplay that stands as the gold standard for the battle royale genre. It offers a level of mechanical depth and character-driven strategy that makes it easy to recommend to anyone looking for a high-skill competitive experience. However, this recommendation now comes with significant reservations because the management of the game seems increasingly disconnected from the actual player experience. There is a visible gap between what the community needs to keep the game healthy and what the "admin" prioritizes, which often feels like a preference for short-term profit over the long-term stability of the competitive environment.

The marketing strategy has become one of the most frustrating aspects of the game's ecosystem. Instead of using its platform to highlight gameplay innovations, engine improvements, or a roadmap for fixing long-standing technical issues, the marketing efforts are almost exclusively funneled into high-priced cosmetic events. When the primary "hype" for a season revolves around expensive heirlooms and crossover skins while fundamental problems like server instability and audio glitches remain unaddressed, it creates a tone-deaf image. This approach makes the player base feel like they are being treated as a revenue stream rather than a community, which eventually erodes the goodwill that the brilliant core gameplay worked so hard to build.

Furthermore, the game’s management appears to rely too heavily on internal metrics while ignoring the qualitative feedback provided by the players. A great game requires a healthy dialogue, yet the current state of Apex suggests that player concerns regarding ranked matchmaking and anti-cheat effectiveness are falling on deaf ears. Marketing should be used to bridge this gap, yet it fails to address the steep learning curve for new players or provide any real assurance that the technical foundation is being modernized. For Apex Legends to truly reach its full potential, the decision-makers must align their marketing and development priorities with the actual health of the game, shifting the focus away from monetization and back toward the polish and transparency that made the game a hit in the first place.

review:
After OVER 1,000 hours of gameplay, I can confidently say the matchmaking is garbage.
The servers, when they work, are okay.
The gambling for in game skins is predatory.
If the servers are having issues (somewhat often), you can't play the game you put money into *if you put money into skins*.
I get its an older game now, but the player base has dropped drastically.
It honestly seems like the developers got their riches from it and have now run.
Smurf accounts run rampant, reporting does presumably nothing... as they kind of have to do nothing now that the player base is so minimal.

It used to be a great game.
It could be a great game again...
But like all the other dying legends, it won't.
They won't fix the game issues, they won't fix the matchmaking, they won't fix the servers, and they wont fix the smurfing...
They'll just take their riches and run.

review:
This game is basically unplayable at it's current state with every single ranked lobby either having a team blatantly using cheats such as anti-recoil or just DDoSing the entire lobby. The top predator players are currently going live on twitch and bragging about what cheats they are using with Respawn doing absolutely nothing to stop them.

The ranked matchmaking is actually laughable. Touch Diamond 4 and you will be sent into Master/Pred lobbies in 9/10 matches as if the game has no diamond players to pull from. Diamond 1 and above is reserved only for players who are 3 stacking or blatant cheaters. If you're playing solo and show any sign of intelligent gameplay Respawn will make sure that you are paired with 2 people who have never touched a shooter in their lives for the next business week. 

I saw someone on reddit say the game is currently very well balanced and it was the top comment, I want whatever you are smoking. Are people ever going to catch on to the fact that they overpower guns and legends every season because they want to sell more skins for that character/gun? will you look me in my eyes with the current state of the Hemlock today and tell me the game is balanced? Game has been out for 7 YEARS by the way.

Don't let my game time fool you, I was here day one playing on console and just recently switched to PC. After 7 years and a couple of hiatuses here and there I am finally and completely done with this game. They care about absolutely nothing but the money. If you still support this company and this game you have been brainwashed to oblivion. What they did to this once great game should be studied in history books for years to come.

review:
cool game and uhhh (Apex Legends is one of the most popular and innovative battle royale games in modern gaming. Developed by Respawn Entertainment and released in 2019, it quickly gained worldwide attention for its fast-paced gameplay, unique characters, and team-based mechanics. Unlike many other battle royale games, Apex Legends focuses heavily on cooperation, strategy, and individual character abilities, making it stand out in a crowded genre.

One of the most distinctive features of Apex Legends is its cast of playable characters, known as “Legends.”but duck sex Each Legend has their own personality, backstory, and special abilities. For example, some characters can heal teammates, while others can create defensive shields or scan the environment for enemies. This system encourages players to think strategically about team composition and how different abilities can work together. As a result, teamwork becomes essential for success, rather than just individual skill.

Another important aspect of Apex Legends is its movement system. The game allows players to slide, climb, and move quickly across the map, creating a dynamic and fluid experience. This makes combat more exciting and unpredictable compared to slower-paced games. Players must constantly stay alert, adapt to changing situations, and make quick decisions to survive.

Communication is also a key strength of the game. Apex Legends introduced an advanced “ping system,” which allows players to communicate with teammates without using voice chat. By simply pressing a button, players can mark enemies, suggest locations, or highlight useful items. This feature makes the game more accessible and enjoyable, especially for players who prefer not to use microphones.

In addition, the game’s design and regular updates keep it fresh and engaging. New Legends, weapons, maps, and seasonal events are frequently added, ensuring that players always have something new to explore. The developers actively listen to the community and make improvements based on feedback, which helps maintain a loyal player base.

In conclusion, Apex Legends has redefined the battle royale genre by combining fast gameplay, unique characters, and strong teamwork mechanics. Its innovative features, such as the ping system and diverse Legends, make it both accessible and strategically deep. As the game continues to evolve, it remains a top choice for players around the world and a major influence on the future of multiplayer gaming)

review:
To be honest, I wish I would have played this game sooner.

I had heard about this game in 2022 in the spring as my younger cousin was playing it with his friends at the time. 

I just installed Apex and played for the first time in the first week of December last year with my buddy, and we've done surprisingly well winning twice in both Duos and Trios in our first 4 weeks of playing, and several more times since then.

All the characters you can play as, or "Legends" as they're called hence the name Apex Legends, are tons of fun to mess around with and their different Tactical & Ultimate Abilities. 

I've played 2 of the other popular Battle Royale games previously (Fortnite and Call of Duty Warzone) and Apex Legends in my opinion is (in my opinion) the best game in the Battle Royale genre and one of my favourite games period. 

I had severe issues with getting softlocked while loading into a match and frequent lag in general in a match, in the Legend select screen pre-game or at all when I started playing in December, then after December I stepped away from the game for just over a month, now halfway through Season 28 and the issues have gone away, which I am happy to see.

Just short of 100 hours of playtime currently and I see myself easily playing for another 100 hours, and even more.

Lifeline is my favourite Legend to play as so far.

If you have not played Apex Legends or any Battle Royale game before, I recommend this.

Even if you're not very good, getting 15th every game, losing immediately upon landing, etc. etc. You can still have fun with it, and you will get better with time. I recommend.

review:
I really wanted to enjoy Apex Legends, but after countless hours, the problems are impossible to ignore.

- Graphics: For a game that prides itself on being visually striking, the textures often look muddy, character models glitch, and optimization is inconsistent. Even on decent hardware, frame drops and stuttering are far too common.

- Gameplay: The gunplay feels unbalanced, with certain weapons dominating while others are practically useless. Movement mechanics, once praised, now feel clunky compared to newer titles. Bugs like hit registration issues and audio dropouts ruin immersion and cost matches.

- Matchmaking: This is where the game truly falls apart. New players are constantly thrown against veterans and smurfs, making matches feel unfair and discouraging. Queue times can be long, and when you finally get in, the skill disparity is glaring.

- Ranking System: Completely broken. Progression feels arbitrary, with wins barely moving the needle while losses punish disproportionately. The system rewards grinding rather than skill, and cheaters or exploiters only make climbing more frustrating.

Overall, Apex Legends feels like a game that had potential but is now weighed down by neglect and poor design choices. Unless Respawn seriously addresses these issues, it’s hard to recommend this to anyone looking for a fair, polished, and enjoyable competitive shooter.

Verdict: Not worth the frustration.

Game was fun but didn't hook me for very long. I have no idea what the game is like in the current year.

review:
Apex Legends Review (from someone who has seen the void)

I launched Apex thinking “yeah this’ll be fun, quick games, maybe some laughs.”
Fast forward 3 hours and I’m staring at my screen like I just got emotionally scammed.

This game doesn’t just take your time — it takes your soul, runs off the map with it, and then pings it like “enemy here.”

Every match is the same:

Land
Loot for 20 minutes like a raccoon in a trash can
Get one-clipped by a guy who hasn’t seen sunlight since 2019
Teammates disappear like my will to live

Also, why does everyone move like they drank 14 Red Bulls and unlocked ultra instinct? I blink and suddenly I’m dead, my loot is gone, and my teammates are arguing in three different languages.

Audio? Nonexistent.

review:
Apex Legends is one of the best AAA battle royales so far. Don't know what is happening there right now.

I like short sessions, good art direction, easy looting without checking internal wiki to understand how to use a things in your bag. Apex fits perfectly into that.
You jump in, play a couple of matches, get your emotions and logoff without feeling like you need to live in the game.

A big part of why it works so well is the foundation from Titanfall 2. You can feel it in everything — gunplay, movement, overall flow. That’s what hooked me back then after Titanfall 2 on PS.

I’ve played around 500 hours in Apex back on Origin, and I don’t regret a single minute.

The characters are also very distinct and memorable. A lot of the events were built around them, which made the game feel more alive and actually interesting to follow. Some of you remember the massacrers on a yacht.

It’s honestly one of those rare moments when Electronic Arts just released a game and got it right.

Yeah, like any game, it eventually starts to feel a bit repetitive. But overall — it still feels like the best battle royale available today.

Highly recommended.

review:
every update brings me closer to crippling depression.

the game was AMAZING back in like seasons 1–10. back when fights felt fair, movement felt godlike, and you didn’t need a phd in “getting third-partied by unemployed demons” just to survive 30 seconds.

now? they’re just being EA. monetization cranked to MAXIMUM OVERDRIVE. $40 skins, $200 heirlooms, and events that feel like they were designed in a boardroom by a guy whose only gaming experience is candy crush. 

and gameplay? oh yeah, that thing we used to care about?

the gundam event is literally just wildcard with a STUPID GUN THAT SHOOTS LIKE A KAMEHAMEHA FROM Dragon Ball. HOW AM I SUPPOSED TO DODGE WHEN YOU’RE HITTING HALF THE LOBBY PER SHOT??? bro is not aiming, he’s just declaring bankruptcy on my entire squad.

but don’t worry, it gets worse.

* matchmaking is a social experiment. i queue solo and get two level 30s while fighting a coordinated trio of apex predators who communicate telepathically.
* ranked is basically hide-and-seek simulator now. you don’t fight, you just crouch behind a rock until top 3 and pray. 
* servers run on a hamster named greg. lag, rubberbanding, packet loss—pick your poison. 
* audio is still playing hide and seek. enemy footsteps? optional feature. 
* cheaters??? oh they’re not cheating, they just have “enhanced awareness of your exact location at all times.” totally legit. 

reddit players are literally saying stuff like:

> “matchmaking is still total trash… game is sweatier than ever”

and honestly? yeah. it’s not even a battle royale anymore, it’s a full-time job. if you don’t play 8 hours a day, you log in just to get farmed by a bald wraith with 80,000 kills and anger issues.

also the meta? don’t worry about picking your favorite legend. just play whoever respawn accidentally overtuned this week before they nerf them into the shadow realm next patch. 

and content? somehow we get BOTH:

* “not enough new content”
* AND “too much useless garbage nobody asked for” 

truly revolutionary game design.


but here’s the worst part.

when the game actually works?
when there’s no lag, no cheaters, no broken weapons, no matchmaking insanity?

it’s STILL one of the best shooters ever made.

and that’s why this hurts so much.

because every update feels like watching something you love slowly get turned into a microtransaction vending machine with occasional gunfights.

10/10 would get third-partied again 👍

review:
I'm a casual player that loves the movement, abilities and gunplay of this game, but abhors the sound.

The movement is top notch and very fun once you figure out how to control your characters momentum, resulting in some very exciting close/mid-range fire fights. This is honestly the best part about Apex.

The active and passive abilites give each character a unique feel, but some characters have clearly been neglected over the years, while others seem to constantly recieve buffs to keep their play rate up. 
(probably to maintain skin sales at the cost of making low play rate characters less fun to play) 

Unfortunately, this also carries over to some of the guns (the Charge rifle and Mastiff have low usage rates but haven't been tuned in quite a while) The gun play feels great ,but some of the guns themselves feel like ass. (why would I ever take a charge rifle when I can just grab a Sentinel and charge it for 150+ damage headshots and not have to deal with the cumbersome charge mechanic for virually no reward).

Lets move on to the worst part of the game; the sound really sucks.
Nearby foot steps have no depth to them, Someone walking on top of a two storey metal building sounds very similar to someone walking a few feet around the corner. 
Some abilities will also fail to play any audio what so ever, especially if there are a lot of teams fighting in the same area in combination with ambient sounds coming from the environment.

Another problem is that some maps really need additional cover to make ring rotations less "♥♥♥♥ you in the ass" punishing. These maps (Olympus and some sections of Worlds Edge in particular) often have long lengths of nothingness where the game turns into "run in serpentine" simulator with little room to fire back at your assailant. The best parts of this game come from the movement so these sections don't feel very fun even when you're the one shooting fish in a barrel.

Anyways, I'm just a casual player so I really can't comment about the status of high rank play ,but a lot of the complaints about this game seem to stem from this area and seem valid.

Last thing, the game is kinda rough on new players, There's a lot to take in and the tuturial skips things like craft stations, what the ♥♥♥♥ EVO is (its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ exp just call it that), and how to get the most out of the games amazing movement. Expanding the firing range to include more of these actions or having specific stages or sections dedicated to practicing more nuanced movement would help a great deal in this regard.

Sincerely, your boy Urban.

review:
Apex Legends is a solid battle royale game that is fun to play, especially with friends. It has a unique system of characters called Legends, each with their own abilities, which adds more strategy compared to other games in the genre.

The gameplay is smooth and fast-paced, and the movement feels really good. The ping system is also very useful, making it easier to communicate with teammates even without a microphone.

However, the game can be a bit difficult for new players, especially when playing against more experienced opponents. Sometimes matchmaking doesn’t feel very balanced, which can make matches frustrating.

The graphics are good, and the maps are well designed, but the game can feel repetitive after a while if you play a lot.

Overall, Apex Legends is a decent game. It has some great features and is enjoyable, but it also has a few issues that can take away from the experience.

review:
i spent money on this game buying their battle pass and this is the garbage i get in return.

the servers are trash, the performance is inconsistent, and the update system is a complete joke. every time there is some small patch, steam ends up showing tens of gigabytes being processed like i am reinstalling half the game again. this time it showed around 49 gb for an update that was nowhere near that size in actual new content. for people with normal internet and limited storage, that is insane.

i already had the game installed, i had played it recently, and i still got hit with this stupid bloated patching process. hours wasted for no good reason. this is not respectful of players’ time, bandwidth, storage, or money.

ea and respawn are always ready to sell battle passes, skins, and cosmetics, but when it comes to basic things like stable servers, efficient patching, and a smooth experience, they clearly do not care enough to fix it. they are happy to take money, but the actual user experience keeps getting treated like an afterthought.

so i uninstalled the game completely.

a game should not feel like a monthly punishment just to keep it updated. i paid money into this and got bad servers, bloated updates, wasted time, and constant frustration back.

not recommended.

here’s an even harsher version:

i spent money on this game and regret it.

the servers suck, the patching system sucks, and the overall experience outside the core gameplay is a complete mess. every update feels like a chore. this latest one pushed me over the edge. steam was showing around 49 gb being processed for a patch that had only a fraction of that in actual new data. that is ridiculous and unacceptable.

i already had the full game installed and had played it recently. instead of a normal update, i got a bloated time-wasting patch process that drags on forever and treats my bandwidth, storage, and time like they are worthless. this kind of garbage might be normal to ea and respawn, but it should not be normal to players.

they keep pushing passes and cosmetics and asking people to spend money, yet they still cannot deliver stable servers and a sane update system. they want live service money without giving live service quality.

i am done with it. i uninstalled the game.

if your game turns every small patch into a giant headache, wastes hours of people’s time, and still cannot provide reliable servers, then it does not deserve a recommendation.

review:
Game can be pretty sweaty and you can encounter cheaters every now and then which is a little annoying but tbh none of that really bothered me that much. 

The reason i'm writing this is because I had incorrectly banned from this game after almost 500 hours of gameplay, under the reasoning of gameplay enhancements. Obviously i first though this was a mistake because ain't know why i'm good enough at this game for people to think i'm cheating right? I appeal for an unban letting them know that it looks to have been done by mistake as i've never used any sort of cheats in a game, most i've ever done was install seamless coop on elden ring. They handle it overnight and unban me by morning. Sweet, right? I boot up the game to find that i'm banned again out of nowhere right after they unbanned me. I haven't even played since i was unbanned, so how could i be banned again before i even boot the game up again? 

Assuming it was mistake, I appealed again. Denied for gameplay enhancement. I appeal again because know i didn't cheat so it must be some issue or bug, right? Denied again. No reasoning beyond "Gameplay Enhancement". No insight, no understanding, no anything. Craziest part was that i was kinda buns at the game but i genuinely enjoyed it. You could look at every single game i've ever played in my life and tell i've never cheated a single game or a single instance. All the money and game time spent just gone.

Hate to be that salty guy but I can't recommend this game if this something that can happen to you after 400 hours of gameplay without proper reason. You don't have to believe me or whatever but whoever is looking into getting this game, I definitely recommend reading this and thinking to yourself how you would feel if this happened to you.

review:
I have been playing Apex Legends for 3+ years.

At this point, it’s not a game.
It’s a lifestyle. A belief system. A legally binding relationship.

People ask me what other games I play.

Other games???

You mean side quests??

I don’t cheat on Apex. I stay loyal.
Through the pain. Through the suffering. Through getting one-clipped by someone whose username looks like a WiFi password.

I have tried to leave. I downloaded other games.
They felt… wrong.

No anxiety when looting.
No fear of getting third-partied every 7 seconds.
No random teammate screaming into the mic like we’re in the ALGS Championship.

Disgusting. Uninstalled immediately.

Let me be clear:

I am not good at this game.

I am what professionals call
“free RP.”

If you see me in your lobby, congratulations.
You are about to have the best game of your life.

My aim is so advanced, I miss shots people didn’t even know were possible to miss.

If missing bullets was a ranked mode,
I’d be #1 Predator worldwide.

My movement?
Imagine a refrigerator learning how to parkour.

My game sense is incredible though.
I can accurately predict the exact moment I’m about to die.

(Spoiler: it’s right after I say “this is the game.”)

Looting phase: billionaire mindset.
Combat phase: homeless.

I will spend 15 minutes crafting the perfect loadout
just to donate it to the first enemy I meet like it’s a charity event.

My teammates are always one of the following:

A literal esports prodigy who 1v3s squads while I’m still choosing a backpack
A guy who landed in a different country for “positioning”
Or someone who disconnects the moment they get emotionally challenged

And then there’s me:
Pinging “I need ammo” with 8 bullets and blind confidence.

Enemies, however, are built different.

These people do NOT miss.
I get beamed so fast I start checking if I accidentally queued into a documentary about suffering.

Matchmaking in this game doesn’t build skill.
It builds character.

And trauma.

And somehow…

I love this game more than any other game in existence.

No other game has ever had this kind of hold on me.
This game has ruined my aim, my sleep schedule, my mental stability, and I still come back like:

“one more game.”

I don’t play Apex because I enjoy it.

I play Apex because I believe in it.

Deep down, in my heart, I know…

One day…

I will win a fight.

10/10
Would get absolutely destroyed again.
See you on the dropship.

review:
👍 Recommended

I keep uninstalling it… and somehow I always come back.

Apex Legends is just one of those games that feels really good to play. Movement is smooth, gunplay is clean, and when everything clicks with your squad, it’s honestly one of the most fun battle royales out there.

That said… yeah, it can be frustrating. Random teammates, getting third-partied every fight, and those games where you loot for 10 minutes just to get deleted in 2 seconds 😅

But then you have that one match where everything goes perfectly, and suddenly you remember why you keep playing.

It’s not perfect, but it’s addicting as hell.

10/10 would get third-partied again.
