review:
"So when do you get better at the game?"
"That's the neat part, you don't !"
The sooner you make peace with this, the less abuse your keyboard and mouse will get.

I hate EA to my core, but Apex is one of their games that truly binds me to this toxic relationship. I've been playing since season 2 and it was interesting to see the game evolve over the years.

Sure I do miss the old Apex and that's the nostalgia talking. But after taking a hiatus and finally getting back in the game, I can say for sure its fun! (lets just ignore ranked for now) 
I don't play multiplayer games alone, but Apex is the only exception. Its infinitely more fun with friends. The movement is so fast paced and smooth, it really hits all the right spots for me when I play, compared to games like CSGO or Valorant. The legends with their unique abilities and skills make the game more complex and interesting.

There are buts too, cheater problem persists, but thankfully I've just encountered a handful and being put in a lobby full of sweats, just makes you want to throw your PC at Mach f#ck at the wall.
And I don't know whether its just me, but launching Apex through Steam is comparatively buggy. Just lmk if you've had the same problem.

But overall, it’s a great game. I just hope it lives long enough not to become EA-fied into oblivion.
Cheers!

1 cup granulated sugar
1 tsp vanilla extract
3 large eggs
1 cup sour cream (optional, for extra creaminess)
¼ cup heavy cream (optional for richness)
Directions:
Prepare the Crust:
Preheat the oven to 325°F (163°C).
In a medium bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs, sugar, melted butter, and a pinch of salt. Stir until the mixture is well combined and the crumbs are moistened.
Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan to form an even layer. Use the back of a spoon or a flat object to press it firmly down.
Bake for 10-12 minutes or until the crust is golden brown. Remove from the oven and let it cool while you prepare the filling.
Prepare the Filling:
In a large bowl, beat the softened cream cheese with a hand mixer or stand mixer on medium speed until smooth and creamy.
Add the sugar and vanilla extract and mix until well combined.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Make sure to scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed.
Mix in the sour cream and heavy cream until the filling is smooth and well combined.
Bake the Cheesecake:
Pour the cream cheese mixture over the cooled crust.
Tap the pan gently on the counter to release any air bubbles.
Bake at 325°F (163°C) for 50-60 minutes or until the center is set but still slightly wobbly. A toothpick or knife inserted should come out mostly clean with a few moist crumbs.
Turn off the oven and let the cheesecake cool in the oven with the door slightly ajar for about an hour to prevent cracking.
Cool and Chill:
Once the cheesecake has cooled to room temperature, refrigerate it for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow it to fully set.
Serve:
When ready to serve, you can top the cheesecake with fresh fruit, fruit compote, or a drizzle of caramel or chocolate sauce, if desired.

review:
Apex Legends is hands-down one of the most polished and exciting shooters in the battle royale space — and even in 2022, it still shines. From the moment you drop into the map, it feels smart, responsive, and full of momentum.

The gunplay is satisfying and crisp, with each weapon feeling distinct and balanced. Movement is fluid and smooth — sliding, climbing, and zip-lining make traversal fun and strategic, not just a gimmick. Every match feels like a fresh opportunity to outplay your opponents with skill, strategy, and teamwork.

The legends themselves are what really set this apart. Each one has personality, unique abilities, and synergy that actually matters. Playing as Lifeline feels completely different than playing as Wraith or Gibraltar, and building a squad that complements each other is rewarding and strategic.

Communication is fantastic (especially with ping-based interaction), so even if you’re playing with randoms, the game sets you up to coordinate and win together. It lowers the barrier to teamwork while still rewarding it in big ways.

The maps are well-designed and evolving, with dynamic events and thoughtful points of interest that keep gameplay feeling fresh. Seasonal updates, ranked modes, and new content keep the experience lively and engaging long after the initial drop.

In a crowded genre, Apex Legends stands tall — fast-paced, polished, and fun whether you’re grinding ranked or just jumping in for a quick match.

10/10 — skillful, strategic, and ridiculously entertaining.

review:
Apex Legends is one of the best free‑to‑play battle royales out there — fast, strategic, and packed with unique characters called Legends, each with distinctive abilities that add real tactical depth to every match. Whether you’re diving into a Trio, Duos, or limited‑time mode, the gameplay feels tight and satisfying, with great gunplay and smart design that keeps you coming back for more.

The ping system alone is brilliant — you can communicate effectively with your squad without a mic, marking enemies, loot, or strategies instantly, which makes even random matchmaking feel coordinated.

Matches are intense, the maps are well built, and the seasonal content with new Legends, weapons, and battle passes keeps the experience fresh.

That said, there are some serious frustrations players often talk about:
🔹 Cheaters still show up — wallhacks, aimbots, and hacks are reported frequently in ranked and high‑skill matches, and players feel the anti‑cheat could be more effective.
🔹 Bot‑like players or smurf accounts can sometimes mix into games, which weakens the competitive feel and makes it hard to judge if a lobby is legit or not.

review:
[h1]Apex Legends is a great game ruined by its matchmaking.[/h1]
[b]Every solo queue match feels like a 1v3v57 while your teammates sprint into fights with no shields, no awareness, and zero interest in teamwork. Pings are ignored, rotations don’t exist, and somehow you’re expected to carry while reviving people who instantly throw themselves back into danger. On top of that, you’re also fighting the servers—Australian servers are basically dead, with no one playing on them past 3pm, so you’re dumped into Singapore lobbies instead where high ping turns every gunfight into a coin flip. The movement and gunplay are elite, but none of that matters when every match turns into babysitting and lag instead of a competitive shooter. Amazing mechanics, absolutely soul-crushing experience.[/b]

review:
Don't ever start playing this game, UNLESS you always have people to play with who are willing to spend their entire waking hours grinding and sweating the game and nothing else...the most try hard desperate game you could ever possibly play, and not meant for solo players AT ALL.
.....and yet I keep coming back, even as a casual solo player with average skill level...so I guess that says something. The core mechanics are still unmatched, even 7 years after release. No other game has movement and gunplay that feels quite as good as Apex when everything clicks. On the other hand, the server quality is still just as HORRIBLE as it was in season 1, and the directional audio is still just as inconsistent, and still randomly drops footstep audio completely at least once a game. You will get shot and die a full second after getting behind cover at least once per game. 
The fact that they still haven't spent the time & money to upgrade to higher quality servers, even after the literal BILLIONS of dollars the game has made for them, is INEXCUSABLE. If it wasn't for that I would still give the game a thumbs up and recommend it, but I cannot in good faith reward that kind of greed. I still play the game, but I stopped spending $$$ on it many seasons ago

I've got over 10k hours on this game casual/sweaty. If you are new to the game or intend to play casually, just to get on ranked. I've always been pretty good at shooters. But streaming has made the average gamer into a sweaty player. And the matchmaking almost never matches you with similarly skilled teammates. Expect to carry if you're any kind of good. 
Now, if you're a skilled shooter who likes to be challenged and has a 3 man squad, there's really no better game than Apex. From shooting to movement, it's tough. Unless you're the average controller player that has a grasp of their Aim Assist, then you'll just one clip everyone at close range.

review:
The Bottom Line: Apex Legends remains a top-tier battle royale that stands apart through its masterful blend of fluid movement, character-driven abilities, and skill-first gunplay. Continuous seasonal evolution and a rich, expanding lore built on Titanfall’s foundation keep the experience fresh and engaging.

What Sets Apex Apart
Legend-Based Gameplay: The roster of distinct characters (Legends) elevates Apex beyond the standard blank-slate battle royale. Their unique abilities encourage strategic team synergy, such as combining Caustic's smoke with Bloodhound's tracking vision, without rigidly defining roles like a traditional hero shooter.

Unmatched Movement & Gunplay: Inheriting Titanfall’s DNA, Apex features the genre's smoothest and most satisfying movement. Sliding, climbing, and momentum-based traversal feel incredible. Gunplay prioritizes skill over loot luck; every weapon is viable from the start, and attachments modify handling rather than just boosting damage.

Progressive Combat Systems: The Evo Shield system brilliantly rewards aggression. You level up your armor by dealing damage, incentivizing early fights and making victories feel more earned throughout the match, rather than punishing you for engaging.

Evolving Content & World
Living Lore: The narrative, set 30 years after Titanfall 2, progresses meaningfully through seasonal events, comics, and cinematics. This deepens the world and character backstories without intruding on moment-to-moment gameplay.

Seasonal Refresh: Regular seasons introduce new Legends, weapons, and map changes. Frequent weapon meta shifts (like rotating which guns are in supply drops) keep the tactical landscape from growing stale, even if it requires ongoing adaptation.

Persistent Flaws
Audio Bugs: The most consistent issue is unreliable audio cues, particularly missing enemy footsteps. This can lead to unfair, surprise engagements that feel out of place in such a polished tactical shooter.

Balance Growing Pains: While constant updates are welcome, some weapon and Legend balance changes can feel jarring, briefly disrupting established playstyles before the meta settles.

Final Thoughts
Apex Legends successfully marries the high-speed, skill-based action of Titanfall with the strategic depth of a hero-based battle royale. Its commitment to rewarding skillful play, fostering team synergy, and consistently evolving its world and gameplay solidify its position as a genre leader. Despite some nagging audio issues, it delivers an exhilarating and uniquely dynamic experience that is difficult to match.

review:
With over 5k hours on console and after 400 hours on pc (with long breaks) this is my pros and cons list, summary, and recommendation

Pros: amazing mechanics (movement feels amazing), good gunplay, high skill ceiling, satisfying, has both a casual and competitive community, skill based and feels rewarding (if you dont cheat)

Cons: soft cheaters are VERY common, bad skill based matchmaking, not new player friendly, most things that were an achievement a few years back (4k and 20 bomb badges, plus a few of the ranked badges) mean nothing now, and laggy servers (especially in the peak hours between 7-10pm local time)

Overall the game has been stepping in a good direction in the past few months, plenty of bugs and issues are being fixed making the game very satisfying to play. Good balance changes have been rolled out recently, with the "overpowered" items being not that annoying to go against at the very least. I always have fun playing because im a very competitive person and the high skill ceiling allows me to have the constant feeling of improvement. One of my favorite games of all time, however the cheating situation in mid-high ranked lobbies (Typically diamond and above) and the obvious greed is slowly killing the game as more and more people are quitting. If the devs can figure out how to combat zens, titans, etc, then the game would feel much more refreshing. It also feels at times that you have to be good at this game to have fun.

I would recommend this game if you are already very familiar with fps games and you have the time to practice. If you are new or bad at fps games or simply dont have the time to keep up with the game and practice then I would recommend you try a different shooter game to start off with. (Games like Marvel Rivals, Overwatch, some COD games, and Fortnite are much more forgiving in this sense)

Strong team-oriented design

What Needs Improvement

Final Thoughts:
Apex Legends remains one of the most mechanically satisfying multiplayer shooters available.

review:
Apex Legends is very hit and miss, but certainly a fun game when its good.

For serious competitive players, Apex is a massive ''dont play''. For casual competitive players there is a lot to love and a lot to hate.

[b]The Good;[/b]
Apex Legends has some of the most satisfying gunplay of any shooter, weapons feel different from one another and punchy. Every bullet that hits you can feel and hear the feedback of, and there is clear recoil and spread on each weapon that you can master or reduce with attachments.
Movement feels good but sometimes clunky, this depends on the map you play in. In general it feels great, jumping sliding climbing its smooth for the most part. And when reloading a weapon you can still climb and move, and even take a zipline. Giving little downtime between movement and shooting.
Maps are very detailed and good looking, lots of cover/buildings/elevations. And with the addition of Arsenals you know where you can guarantee a weapon you like to play with.
Character abilities are fun to use and with the exception of the teleport abilities, are often unique and varried. Each character excels in a certain playstyle and will preform better on some maps and worse on others.
Guns are in a surprisingly balanced state, there are only a few guns that are unbalanced as of writing this so most are viable and worth playing with.

[b]The Bad[/b]
The servers of Apex Legends are clearly something Electronic Arts has not spend much effort or money on. There are many servers that need lagcompensation to even work in the first place, and there is often delays of 1 second or straight up lag and packageloss. This is often playable if you just play the game for fun, but if you play with tournaments in mind or want to push yourself to your limits as a competitive player... the servers are horrible.
The result of poor servers is; Being hit by bullets when you have been in cover for a full second already, The game picking up loot and weapons that you havent even hovered over as lagcompensation guesses what you would have done, Abilities and movement not working as packageloss does not registrate everything.
Map balance is something that is not great on some maps, but good on others. Some maps have very inconsistent loot in different POI's meaning you can drop and have almost nothing to fight with, while another time you have so much you cant take it all with you. Some maps have very poor viewlines, allowing sniping from accross the map, and huge open areas with not so much as a box or car to hide behind. Mostly its decent enough though.
The matchmaking is very bad, putting players who dont want to play together in forced teams of 3. Toxic console players together with pc players, controller players with aim assist and cheats together with mouse and keyboard. The ban and block system also does not work, since and it will put players that you block in your team regardless.
Most people also use voicecoms only to trashtalk their teammates after they die from a dumb mistake they make, and toxic behaviour does not get punished.
There is no way to filter out bad and toxic players in any way, and this really enforces toxic behaviour from the playerbase. After all it does not matter what you say in voicechat or text chat, there are no consequences and you might even get a match with the same people next match.

[b]Bugs;[/b]
Apex Legends is full of bugs, some get patched out while others (re)appear. There is no end to them, and you will always run into some of them from character bugs to item bugs and even straight up misallinged hitboxes on maps.
This makes Apex feel like an Early Access game that will never get into a fully released state, its good enough to play but the annoying bugs always pull it down.

[b]Verdict;[/b]
Overall i recommend trying Apex Legends if you have been sleeping on this game, its has a lot of fun gameplay and costs only time and download space. However if you feel like the game is too toxic/laggy/buggy, dont try and wait it out for it ''to get better''. Some days are unplayable and there is just no way to enjoy it then. I wish i could fully recommend the game, but in its current state is very much ''try it out'' because of how bad the playerbase and matchmaking is.

review:
I love respawn
We hate EA

I’ve been playing since 2019; Apex Legends has been my favorite battle royale since then. Fortnite, Warzone, PUBG… none of them has given me the experience of such a high but reachable skill ceiling. Keeping its core gameplay pretty consistent and balanced even all these years later, after 3k+ hours in this game, I’d like to talk about some stuff

[I would like to start with what I love about this game before saying what I don’t like]
- I ABSOLUTELY LOVE THE MOVEMENT IN THIS GAME. I don’t think I need to explain. All these years and you just learned about this game? That’s probably because you just got recommended clips of people moving like crazy around their enemies and doing all types of smooth and epic movements.
- Respawn, the developers, show love and care for this game, making sure to keep the characters and weapons balanced as much as they can. (A lot of times they don’t, but hey… have we ever had one hero-shooter where everything was balanced from the start and forever?). And also, every now and then, coming up with new stuff to try to see what sticks and what doesn’t, the polish they’ve gotten in this game, it’s definitely there.
- Fun roster of characters and weapons. If you’ve played Titanfall 2 (yep, same developers), you would probably recognize a lot of stuff. From weapons, abilities, and even characters from the game! At the moment of writing this review, the amount of characters and weapons isn’t so big as to make it impossible for new players to understand most of them in the first matches. All of them are unique and fun in their own way.
- Active community. No matter how much time passes, you will always find people uploading guides, reviews, opinions, information, tips, tricks… everything. So far, the game has had some dips in the player base, but never something so big that it actually affects it. People really love this game.


[And now, what I don’t like about the game]
>>>EA<<<

If you know about Electronic Arts, you can skip the rest; you already know who they are and how they operate.
If you don’t, well… Let me tell you about them:

EA has made this game a stupid money-making machine, and they aren’t going to stop. With cosmetics costing hundreds of dollars, “””content””” is what they call an event that consists of 20 skins (sometimes (a lot of times) even reskins) and modifying a weapon or gamemode with a quirky gimmick. As for today (Jan 21, 2026), we haven’t had a new legend in 8 months and haven’t had a new weapon in a year (we kinda got an old weapon from TF2 a couple months ago, but it was for an LTM; we haven’t seen it since).
EA has definitely pushed Respawn to more predatory tactics to squeeze as much money as they can from the players, putting aside the real content in the game. 
Also with the firings (probably) because EA wishes to replace everyone with AI and cut as many corners as they can so at the end of the year they can have a 0.03% increase in revenue. I mean, we literally got the latest Legend lore writer fired DAYS AFTER IT WAS LAUNCHED! EA is either stupid or so hungry for money that they can’t seem to think about what players want and love about the game.
Unfortunately, Respawn also has made some decisions of his own that follow what Daddy EA would approve. Not many, but still something that is worrying the player base.

[I'm going to finish with a simple message]
I love every single one of the players in this game. I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hate EA and hope their people eat ♥♥♥♥ or something, and Respawn, you're doing good so far, but don't you dare do anything stupid, please.
label: Negative
